1	\chapter{{\v C}ech computation of higher direct images $R^i f_*$ (unconditional)}
2	\label{chap:Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage}
3	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechHigherDirectImage.lean
4	% SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, \S{\v C}ech cohomology of
5	% quasi-coherent sheaves and \S Quasi-coherence of higher direct images
6	% (Lemmas \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial},
7	% \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero},
8	% \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent},
9	% \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images-application},
10	% \texttt{lemma-flat-base-change-cohomology}) (read from
11	% references/stacks-coherent.tex).
12	
13	\section{Setup and motivation}
14	
15	This chapter constructs the higher derived direct images \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) for
16	\(i \geq 1\) \emph{without appealing to injective resolutions in the category of
17	sheaves of modules}. The companion development of
18	Chapter~\ref{chap:Cohomology_HigherDirectImage} defines \(R^i f_*\) as a right
19	derived functor, which in a formalisation requires the ambient category of
20	\(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules to have enough injectives; that property is not currently
21	available for sheaves of modules over a sheaf of rings whose value category varies
22	over the site. The {\v C}ech approach developed here sidesteps the issue entirely:
23	it computes \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) as the cohomology of an explicit complex built
24	from the pushforwards of \(\mathcal{F}\) over the finite intersections of an affine
25	open cover, and so produces an \emph{unconditional} construction of \(R^i f_*\) for
26	quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{F}\) and separated quasi-compact \(f\).
27	
28	Throughout, \(f : X \to S\) is a morphism of schemes that is quasi-compact and
29	separated (so that all finite intersections of an affine open cover of \(X\) are
30	again affine), and \(\mathcal{F}\) is a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. We
31	write \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\) for the category of quasi-coherent
32	\(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. A base change of \(f\) along a morphism \(g : S' \to S\)
33	is recorded by the cartesian square obtained from the fibre product
34	\(X' = X \times_S S'\):
35	\[
36	  \begin{array}{ccc}
37	    X' & \xrightarrow{\;g'\;} & X \\[2pt]
38	    {\scriptstyle f'}\big\downarrow & & \big\downarrow{\scriptstyle f} \\[2pt]
39	    S' & \xrightarrow{\;g\;} & S
40	  \end{array}
41	\]
42	Here \(f' : X' \to S'\) is the base change of \(f\), \(g' : X' \to X\) is the first
43	projection, and \(\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}\) is the pullback of
44	\(\mathcal{F}\) to \(X'\).
45	
46	\section{The {\v C}ech nerve and {\v C}ech complex of an affine cover}
47	
48	\begin{definition}
49	
50	\leanok
51	[{\v C}ech nerve of an affine open cover]
52	  \label{def:cech_nerve}
53	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.CechNerve}
54	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, \S{\v C}ech cohomology of
55	  % quasi-coherent sheaves, opening paragraph and Lemma
56	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial}
57	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L36--67).
58	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $X$ be a scheme.
59	  % Let $U \subset X$ be an affine open.
60	  % Recall that a {\it standard open covering} of $U$ is a covering
61	  % of the form $\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i = 1}^n D(f_i)$
62	  % where $f_1, \ldots, f_n \in \Gamma(U, \mathcal{O}_X)$ generate
63	  % the unit ideal, see
64	  % Schemes, Definition \ref{schemes-definition-standard-covering}."
65	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, \S{\v C}ech cohomology of
66	  quasi-coherent sheaves.}
67	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and let
68	  \(\mathcal{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be a finite open cover of \(X\) by
69	  affine opens, with index set \(I\). For a finite nonempty tuple
70	  \(i_0, \ldots, i_p \in I\) write
71	  \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = U_{i_0} \cap \cdots \cap U_{i_p}\) for the corresponding
72	  intersection. The \emph{{\v C}ech nerve} of \(\mathcal{U}\) is the augmented
73	  simplicial object in \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\) whose object in simplicial degree
74	  \(p\) is the direct image, along the open immersions
75	  \(j_{i_0 \ldots i_p} : U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \hookrightarrow X\), of the restriction
76	  of \(\mathcal{O}_X\) (or of any chosen quasi-coherent sheaf) to the
77	  \((p+1)\)-fold intersections,
78	  \[
79	    [p] \;\longmapsto\;
80	      \prod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
81	        (j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_* \,\bigl(\,\cdot\,|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}\bigr),
82	  \]
83	  with faces given by the restriction maps that omit one index and degeneracies by
84	  the maps that repeat one index; the augmentation in degree \(-1\) is the object
85	  on all of \(X\). When \(X\) is separated each intersection
86	  \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) is itself affine. The local affine model is the
87	  \emph{standard open covering} \(U = \bigcup_{i=1}^n D(f_i)\) of an affine open
88	  \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) by basic opens, where \(f_1, \ldots, f_n \in A\)
89	  generate the unit ideal; on such a cover the intersections are again basic opens
90	  \(D(f_{i_0} \cdots f_{i_p})\).
91	\end{definition}
92	
93	\begin{definition}
94	
95	\leanok
96	[{\v C}ech complex of a quasi-coherent sheaf]
97	  \label{def:cech_complex}
98	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.CechComplex}
99	  \uses{def:cech_nerve}
100	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
101	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (proof)
102	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L59--77).
103	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Clearly the {\v C}ech complex
104	  % $\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})$
105	  % is identified with the complex
106	  % $$
107	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to
108	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}} \to
109	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1i_2} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}f_{i_2}} \to
110	  % \ldots
111	  % $$
112	  % We are asked to show that the extended complex
113	  % \begin{equation}
114	  % \label{equation-extended}
115	  % 0 \to
116	  % M \to
117	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to
118	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}} \to
119	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1i_2} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}f_{i_2}} \to
120	  % \ldots
121	  % \end{equation}
122	  % (whose truncation we have studied in
123	  % Algebra, Lemma \ref{algebra-lemma-cover-module}) is exact."
124	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
125	  \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (standard-cover {\v C}ech
126	  vanishing).}
127	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module and let
128	  \(\mathcal{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be a finite affine open cover with
129	  all intersections \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) affine (e.g.\ \(X\) separated). The
130	  \emph{{\v C}ech complex} \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\)
131	  is the cochain complex obtained by applying the global-sections functor to the
132	  {\v C}ech nerve of Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve} evaluated at \(\mathcal{F}\);
133	  in degree \(p\) it is
134	  \[
135	    \check{\mathcal{C}}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})
136	      = \prod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
137	        \mathcal{F}\bigl(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\bigr),
138	  \]
139	  with differential \(d : \check{\mathcal{C}}^p \to \check{\mathcal{C}}^{p+1}\) the
140	  alternating sum \((d s)_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}} = \sum_{j=0}^{p+1} (-1)^j\,
141	  s_{i_0 \ldots \widehat{i_j} \ldots i_{p+1}}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}}}\) of the
142	  restriction maps. Over an affine \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) with
143	  \(\mathcal{F}|_U = \widetilde{M}\) and a standard cover by the
144	  \(D(f_i)\), this is the complex
145	  \(\prod_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to \prod_{i_0 i_1} M_{f_{i_0} f_{i_1}} \to \cdots\) of
146	  localisations. The \emph{relative {\v C}ech complex} \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet
147	  (\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\) for \(f : X \to S\) is the analogous complex in
148	  \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\) obtained by applying \(f_*\) over each finite
149	  intersection instead of global sections; its terms are
150	  \(\prod_{i_0 \ldots i_p} (f|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}})_*
151	  \bigl(\mathcal{F}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}\bigr)\), and it is a complex in
152	  \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\) because each \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) is affine and the
153	  pushforward of a quasi-coherent sheaf along a quasi-compact quasi-separated
154	  morphism is quasi-coherent.
155	\end{definition}

157	\section{Affine acyclicity of the {\v C}ech complex}
158	
159	\begin{lemma}
160	
161	\leanok
162	[{\v C}ech acyclicity on affines]
163	  \label{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}
164	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.CechAcyclic.affine}
165	  \uses{def:cech_complex}
166	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Tag 02KG (affine vanishing);
167	  % Lemma \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (standard-cover
168	  % {\v C}ech vanishing) and Lemma
169	  % \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero} (Serre vanishing on affines)
170	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L44--52 and L145--155).
171	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $X$ be a scheme.
172	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
173	  % Let $\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i = 1}^n D(f_i)$ be a standard
174	  % open covering of an affine open of $X$.
175	  % Then $\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0$ for
176	  % all $p > 0$."
177	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Serre vanishing: Higher cohomology vanishes on affine schemes
178	  % for quasi-coherent modules.
179	  % Let $X$ be a scheme.
180	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
181	  % For any affine open $U \subset X$ we have
182	  % $H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0$ for all $p > 0$."
183	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
184	  \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (standard-cover {\v C}ech
185	  vanishing) and Stacks, Tag 02KG
186	  (\texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero}, Serre vanishing on
187	  affines).}
188	  Let \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) be an affine scheme, \(\mathcal{F}\) a
189	  quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_U\)-module, and
190	  \(\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i=1}^n D(f_i)\) a standard open cover (so the
191	  \(f_i \in A\) generate the unit ideal). Then the {\v C}ech complex
192	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\) has vanishing cohomology
193	  in all positive degrees:
194	  \[
195	    \check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0 \qquad \text{for all } p > 0,
196	  \]
197	  and consequently \(H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0\) for all \(p > 0\).
198	\end{lemma}
199

201	% In other words, $f_1, \ldots, f_n$ are elements of $A$
202	% which generate the unit ideal of $A$.
203	% Write $\mathcal{F}|_U = \widetilde{M}$ for some $A$-module $M$.
204	% ...
205	% It suffices to show that (\ref{equation-extended})
206	% is exact after localizing at a prime $\mathfrak p$, see
207	% Algebra, Lemma \ref{algebra-lemma-characterize-zero-local}.
208	% In fact we will show that the extended complex localized
209	% at $\mathfrak p$ is homotopic to zero.
210	% There exists an index $i$ such that $f_i \not \in \mathfrak p$.
211	% Choose and fix such an element $i_{\text{fix}}$. ...
212	% Let us define a homotopy
213	% $$
214	% h :
215	% \prod\nolimits_{i_0 \ldots i_{p + 1}}
216	% M_{f_{i_0} \ldots f_{i_{p + 1}}, \mathfrak p}
217	% \longrightarrow
218	% \prod\nolimits_{i_0 \ldots i_p}
219	% M_{f_{i_0} \ldots f_{i_p}, \mathfrak p}
220	% $$
221	% by the rule
222	% $$
223	% h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = s_{i_{\text{fix}} i_0 \ldots i_p}
224	% $$
225	% ... We compute ... $(dh + hd)(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = s_{i_0 \ldots i_p}$ ...
226	% This proves the identity map is homotopic to zero as desired."
227	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "We are going to apply
228	% Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-cech-vanish-basis}.
229	% As our basis $\mathcal{B}$ for the topology of $X$ we are going to use
230	% the affine opens of $X$. ... Note that the intersection of standard opens
231	% in an affine is another standard open. Hence property (1) holds. ...
232	% Finally, condition (3) of the lemma follows from
233	% Lemma \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial}."
234	\begin{proof}
235	  \uses{def:cech_complex}
236	  Write \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) and \(\mathcal{F}|_U = \widetilde{M}\) for an
237	  \(A\)-module \(M\). By Definition~\ref{def:cech_complex} the {\v C}ech complex of
238	  the standard cover is the complex of localisations
239	  \[
240	    \prod_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \;\to\;
241	    \prod_{i_0 i_1} M_{f_{i_0} f_{i_1}} \;\to\;
242	    \prod_{i_0 i_1 i_2} M_{f_{i_0} f_{i_1} f_{i_2}} \;\to\; \cdots,
243	  \]
244	  and the claim \(\check{H}^p = 0\) for \(p > 0\) is equivalent to exactness of the
245	  extended complex \(0 \to M \to \prod_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to \cdots\). Exactness may
246	  be checked after localising at an arbitrary prime \(\mathfrak{p} \subset A\).
247	  Choose an index \(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) with
248	  \(f_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}} \notin \mathfrak{p}\); then
249	  \(M_{f_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}}, \mathfrak{p}} = M_{\mathfrak{p}}\), and the
250	  prescription \(h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = s_{i_{\mathrm{fix}} i_0 \ldots i_p}\)
251	  defines a contracting homotopy of the localised extended complex: a direct
252	  computation gives \((dh + hd)(s) = s\), so the identity is homotopic to zero and
253	  the localised complex is exact. As exactness holds after every localisation, the
254	  extended complex is exact and \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = 0\) for
255	  \(p > 0\).

258	  the topology and the standard open covers as the admissible covers. Intersections

260	  covers of a basic open, and the {\v C}ech vanishing just proved supplies the
261	  acyclicity hypothesis; the {\v C}ech-to-cohomology comparison on a basis then
262	  yields \(H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) = 0\) for all \(p > 0\). \emph{(This is Serre
263	  vanishing for quasi-coherent sheaves on affine schemes.)}
264	\end{proof}

266	\section{The {\v C}ech complex computes $R^i f_*$}
267	
268	\begin{lemma}
269	
270	\leanok
271	[{\v C}ech complex computes the higher direct images]
272	  \label{lem:cech_computes_cohomology}
273	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cech_computes_higherDirectImage}
274	  \uses{lem:cech_acyclic_affine, def:cech_complex}
275	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Tag 02KE; Lemma
276	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent} ({\v C}ech computes cohomology when
277	  % all intersections are affine) and Lemma
278	  % \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images-application}
279	  % ($H^q(X,\mathcal{F}) = H^0(S, R^q f_*\mathcal{F})$ for affine $S$)
280	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L245--256 and L843--854).

282	  % Let $\mathcal{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i$ be an open covering such that
283	  % $U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}$ is affine open for all $p \ge 0$ and all
284	  % $i_0, \ldots, i_p \in I$.
285	  % In this case for any quasi-coherent sheaf $\mathcal{F}$ we have
286	  % $$
287	  % \check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = H^p(X, \mathcal{F})
288	  % $$
289	  % as $\Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)$-modules for all $p$."
290	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $f : X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes.
291	  % Assume that $f$ is quasi-separated and quasi-compact.
292	  % Assume $S$ is affine.
293	  % For any quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module $\mathcal{F}$
294	  % we have
295	  % $$
296	  % H^q(X, \mathcal{F}) = H^0(S, R^qf_*\mathcal{F})
297	  % $$
298	  % for all $q \in \mathbf{Z}$."
299	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Tag 02KE
300	  (\texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent}) and
301	  \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images-application}.}
302	  Let \(f : X \to S\) be a separated quasi-compact morphism of schemes, let
303	  \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and let
304	  \(\mathfrak{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be a finite affine open cover of
305	  \(X\) (so, by separatedness, every intersection \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) is
306	  affine). Then the cohomology sheaves of the relative {\v C}ech complex
307	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\) of
308	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_complex} compute the higher direct images: for every
309	  \(i \geq 0\) there is a canonical isomorphism of \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-modules
310	  \[
311	    H^i\bigl(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\bigr)
312	      \;\cong\; R^i f_*\mathcal{F}.
313	  \]
314	  In particular, over an affine base \(S = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\), taking global
315	  sections gives \(H^i(X, \mathcal{F}) = \check{H}^i(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F}) =
316	  H^0(S, R^i f_*\mathcal{F})\) as \(A\)-modules for all \(i\).
317	\end{lemma}
318	
319	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "In view of
320	% Lemma \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero}
321	% this is a special case of
322	% Cohomology, Lemma
323	% \ref{cohomology-lemma-cech-spectral-sequence-application}."
324	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Consider the Leray spectral sequence
325	% $E_2^{p, q} = H^p(S, R^qf_*\mathcal{F})$
326	% converging to $H^{p + q}(X, \mathcal{F})$, see
327	% Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-Leray}.
328	% By Lemma \ref{lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images}
329	% we see that the sheaves $R^qf_*\mathcal{F}$ are quasi-coherent.
330	% By Lemma \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero}
331	% we see that $E_2^{p, q} = 0$ when $p > 0$.
332	% Hence the spectral sequence degenerates at $E_2$ and we win."
333	\begin{proof}
334	  \uses{lem:cech_acyclic_affine, def:cech_complex}
335	  The question is local on \(S\), and the formation of both sides commutes with
336	  restriction to opens of \(S\), so we may assume \(S = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) is
337	  affine. Because \(f\) is separated and quasi-compact, each finite intersection
338	  \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) is affine, and \(f\) restricted to it is an affine
339	  morphism; by the affine acyclicity of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} the
340	  higher direct images of \(\mathcal{F}\) over each such intersection vanish, so the
341	  terms of the relative {\v C}ech complex carry no hidden higher cohomology.
342	
343	  Consider the {\v C}ech-to-cohomology spectral sequence of the cover
344	  \(\mathfrak{U}\), with \(E_2\)-page
345	  \(E_2^{p,q} = \check{H}^p(\mathfrak{U}, \underline{H}^q(\mathcal{F}))\)
346	  converging to \(H^{p+q}(X, \mathcal{F})\). Since all intersections are affine,
347	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} forces the presheaf cohomology
348	  \(\underline{H}^q(\mathcal{F})\) to vanish on the intersections for \(q > 0\), so
349	  the spectral sequence collapses to its row \(q = 0\): the {\v C}ech cohomology of
350	  \(\mathcal{F}\) for the cover \(\mathfrak{U}\) computes the sheaf cohomology,
351	  \(\check{H}^p(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F}) = H^p(X, \mathcal{F})\) for all \(p\),
352	  and the same identification holds relatively, i.e.\ the cohomology sheaves of
353	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\) compute the cohomology
354	  sheaves of \(Rf_*\mathcal{F}\).
355	
356	  It remains to identify these cohomology sheaves with \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\). Over
357	  the affine base \(S = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\), the Leray spectral sequence
358	  \(E_2^{p,q} = H^p(S, R^q f_*\mathcal{F}) \Rightarrow H^{p+q}(X, \mathcal{F})\)
359	  has \(R^q f_*\mathcal{F}\) quasi-coherent, whence \(H^p(S, R^q f_*\mathcal{F}) = 0\)
360	  for \(p > 0\) by Serre vanishing (Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}). The Leray
361	  sequence therefore degenerates at \(E_2\), giving
362	  \(H^i(X, \mathcal{F}) = H^0(S, R^i f_*\mathcal{F})\). Combining with the previous
363	  paragraph, \(H^i\bigl(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\bigr)
364	  \cong R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) as \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-modules for all \(i \geq 0\).
365	\end{proof}

373	  \label{def:cech_higher_direct_image}
374	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechHigherDirectImage}
375	  \uses{lem:cech_computes_cohomology}
376	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
377	  % \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images-application}
378	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L843--854); the unconditional
379	  % packaging via the {\v C}ech complex is an Archon-original construction grounded
380	  % in this lemma and Lemma \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent}.
381	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $f : X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes.
382	  % Assume that $f$ is quasi-separated and quasi-compact.
383	  % Assume $S$ is affine.
384	  % For any quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module $\mathcal{F}$
385	  % we have
386	  % $$
387	  % H^q(X, \mathcal{F}) = H^0(S, R^qf_*\mathcal{F})
388	  % $$
389	  % for all $q \in \mathbf{Z}$."
390	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
391	  \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images-application}; unconditional
392	  {\v C}ech packaging is Archon-original.}
393	  Let \(f : X \to S\) be a separated quasi-compact morphism and \(\mathcal{F}\) a
394	  quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. Fix a finite affine open cover
395	  \(\mathfrak{U}\) of \(X\). The \emph{(unconditional) \(i\)-th higher direct
396	  image} is defined, for each \(i \geq 0\), as the \(i\)-th cohomology sheaf of the
397	  relative {\v C}ech complex,
398	  \[
399	    R^i f_*\mathcal{F} \;:=\;
400	      H^i\bigl(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\bigr)
401	      \in \operatorname{QCoh}(S).
402	  \]
403	  This requires \emph{no} hypothesis that the category of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
404	  have enough injectives: the right-hand side is the cohomology of an explicit
405	  complex of quasi-coherent sheaves. By
406	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_computes_cohomology} the result is canonically isomorphic to
407	  the derived-functor higher direct image wherever the latter is defined, and in
408	  particular is independent of the chosen affine cover \(\mathfrak{U}\) up to
409	  canonical isomorphism: two affine covers admit a common affine refinement, and
410	  the induced refinement maps on {\v C}ech complexes are quasi-isomorphisms because
411	  both compute the same \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) by
412	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_computes_cohomology}. For \(i = 0\) one recovers the ordinary
413	  pushforward \(R^0 f_*\mathcal{F} = f_*\mathcal{F}\).
414	\end{definition}

466	    g^*\bigl(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\bigr) \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;

487	% and that $X_B$ is separated. ...
488	% We have the following relation between the {\v C}ech complexes
489	% $$
490	% \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}_B, \mathcal{F}_B) =
491	% \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B
492	% $$
493	% as follows from

498	  The assertion that the base-change map is an isomorphism is local on \(S'\), so we
499	  may assume \(S = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) and \(S' = \operatorname{Spec}(B)\) with
500	  \(A \to B\) flat. By Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_computes_cohomology} both
501	  \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) and \(R^i f'_*\mathcal{F}'\) are quasi-coherent, associated
502	  to the \(A\)-module \(H^i(X, \mathcal{F})\) and the \(B\)-module
503	  \(H^i(X', \mathcal{F}')\) respectively; since pullback along \(g\) corresponds to
504	  \(- \otimes_A B\) on modules, it suffices to prove that the comparison map
505	  \(H^i(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B \to H^i(X', \mathcal{F}')\) is an isomorphism.
506	
507	  Choose a finite affine open cover \(\mathfrak{U} : X = U_1 \cup \dots \cup U_t\);
508	  by separatedness all intersections \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) are affine. Base
509	  changing the cover yields an affine open cover \(\mathfrak{U}_B\) of the
510	  again-separated \(X' = X_B\), with each \((U_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_B\) affine. By
511	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_computes_cohomology} the two {\v C}ech complexes compute the
512	  respective cohomologies. The affine base change for the \(i = 0\) direct image
513	  identifies each term of the base-changed {\v C}ech complex with the original term
514	  tensored over \(A\) with \(B\), giving an isomorphism of cochain complexes
515	  \[
516	    \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}_B, \mathcal{F}_B)
517	      \;\cong\;
518	      \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B.
519	  \]
520	  Because \(A \to B\) is flat, \(- \otimes_A B\) is exact and commutes with the
521	  formation of cohomology of a complex; applying \(H^i\) for any \(i \geq 0\) yields
522	  \[
523	    H^i(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
524	      H^i(X', \mathcal{F}').
525	  \]
526	  Translating back to sheaves on \(S' = \operatorname{Spec}(B)\) — using that the
527	  unconditional higher direct images are the quasi-coherent sheaves associated to
528	  these modules — gives the asserted isomorphism
529	  \(g^*(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}) \xrightarrow{\sim} R^i f'_*\mathcal{F}'\) for all
530	  \(i \geq 0\). The flatness of \(g\) (hence of \(g'\), by base change) is exactly
531	  what makes \(- \otimes_A B\) exact, and is therefore the precise hypothesis under
532	  which the {\v C}ech computation of Definition~\ref{def:cech_higher_direct_image}
533	  is compatible with base change.
534	\end{proof}

9	/-!
10	# Čech computation of the higher direct images `Rⁱ f_*` (unconditional)
11	
12	This file constructs the higher derived direct images `Rⁱ f_* F` for `i ≥ 1`
13	**without appealing to injective resolutions** in the category of sheaves of
14	modules. The companion `Cohomology/HigherDirectImage.lean` defines `Rⁱ f_*` as a
15	right derived functor, which requires the ambient category of `O_X`-modules to
16	have enough injectives — a property not currently available for sheaves of
17	modules over a sheaf of rings whose value category varies over the site. The
18	Čech approach developed here sidesteps the issue: it computes `Rⁱ f_* F` as the
19	cohomology of an explicit complex built from the pushforwards of `F` over the
20	finite intersections of an affine open cover, producing an **unconditional**
21	construction of `Rⁱ f_*` for quasi-coherent `F` and separated quasi-compact `f`.
22	
23	Throughout, `f : X ⟶ S` is a quasi-compact, separated morphism of schemes (so
24	all finite intersections of an affine open cover of `X` are again affine), and
25	`F : X.Modules` is a quasi-coherent `O_X`-module. A base change of `f` along
26	`g : S' ⟶ S` is recorded by a cartesian square
27	```
28	  X' --g'--> X
29	  |f'        |f
30	  v          v
31	  S' --g---> S
32	```
33	with `F' = (g')^* F` the pullback of `F` to `X'`.
34	
35	The six main declarations are:
36	
37	* `AlgebraicGeometry.CechNerve` — the (augmented) Čech nerve of an affine open
38	  cover, an augmented cosimplicial object of `O_X`-modules.
39	* `AlgebraicGeometry.CechComplex` — the relative Čech complex in `QCoh(S)`, a
40	  cochain complex of `O_S`-modules whose degree-`p` term is the product of the
41	  pushforwards of `F` over the `(p+1)`-fold intersections of the cover.
42	* `AlgebraicGeometry.CechAcyclic.affine` — Čech acyclicity on affines: the Čech
43	  complex of a standard cover of an affine scheme has vanishing cohomology in
44	  all positive degrees (Serre vanishing for quasi-coherent sheaves on affines).
45	* `AlgebraicGeometry.cech_computes_higherDirectImage` — the cohomology of the
46	  relative Čech complex is canonically isomorphic to `Rⁱ f_* F` wherever the
47	  derived functor is defined.
48	* `AlgebraicGeometry.cechHigherDirectImage` — the **unconditional** `i`-th higher
49	  direct image, defined as the `i`-th cohomology sheaf of the relative Čech
50	  complex (no enough-injectives hypothesis required).
51	* `AlgebraicGeometry.cech_flatBaseChange` — flat base change for the
52	  unconditional Čech higher direct images.
53	
54	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage.tex`.
55	
56	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, §Čech cohomology of quasi-coherent
57	sheaves and §Quasi-coherence of higher direct images; Tags 02KE
58	(`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent`), 02KG
59	(`lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero`), 02KH
60	(`lemma-flat-base-change-cohomology`).
61	-/

73	/-- **Čech nerve of an affine open cover** (Stacks, Cohomology of Schemes, §Čech
74	cohomology of quasi-coherent sheaves).
75	
76	For a scheme `X`, a finite affine open cover `𝒰 : X = ⋃ Uᵢ` and a quasi-coherent
77	sheaf `F`, the *Čech nerve* is the augmented cosimplicial object of
78	`O_X`-modules whose object in simplicial degree `p` is the product, over the
79	`(p+1)`-tuples `(i₀,…,i_p)` of indices, of the direct images
80	`(j_{i₀…i_p})_* (F|_{U_{i₀…i_p}})` of the restriction of `F` to the
81	`(p+1)`-fold intersection `U_{i₀…i_p} = U_{i₀} ∩ ⋯ ∩ U_{i_p}` along the open
82	immersion `j_{i₀…i_p} : U_{i₀…i_p} ↪ X`. Faces are the restriction maps that
83	omit one index, degeneracies repeat one index, and the augmentation in degree
84	`-1` is `F` itself on all of `X`. When `X` is separated each intersection
85	`U_{i₀…i_p}` is affine.

88	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`. -/

93	  -- index-omitting restriction maps, degeneracies the index-repeating maps, and

99	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — scheme-level Čech nerve backbone
100	
101	The genuine construction of the {\v C}ech nerve `CechNerve` factors through two
102	ingredients that are independent of one another:
103	
104	* a *geometric* backbone — the augmented {\v C}ech nerve of the cover, an
105	  augmented simplicial **scheme** over `X` (the iterated fibre powers of
106	  `∐ᵢ Uᵢ` over `X`), which exists unconditionally because `Scheme` has all
107	  finite limits; and
108	* a *push-pull* functor `(Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules`, `(Y, p) ↦ p_* p^* F`, that
109	  turns the simplicial scheme over `X` into the cosimplicial `O_X`-module
110	  `CechNerve`.
111	
112	The backbone (`coverArrow`, `coverCechNerve`) is built here axiom-clean. The
113	push-pull functor is the remaining gap: its `map_comp` requires the
114	`pushforwardComp` / `pullbackComp` coherence isomorphisms (the same coherence
115	quagmire active in `Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean`), so `CechNerve` itself is
116	left as the single genuine hole.
117	
118	Independently of the nerve, the passage *from* an augmented cosimplicial
119	`O_X`-module *to* the relative {\v C}ech cochain complex in `QCoh(S)` is pure,
120	coherence-free plumbing (`relativeCechComplexOfNerve`): forget the augmentation,
121	push forward along `f` via `CosimplicialObject.whiskering`, and take the
122	alternating coface-map cochain complex. We record it here so that `CechComplex`
123	is *defined* in terms of `CechNerve` — closing `CechNerve` axiom-clean
124	immediately yields an axiom-clean `CechComplex`. -/
125	
126	/-- The arrow `∐ᵢ Uᵢ ⟶ X` (`Sigma.desc 𝒰.f`) attached to an open cover `𝒰` of a
127	scheme `X`. Its augmented {\v C}ech nerve is the geometric backbone of the
128	relative {\v C}ech complex. Project-local: packages the cover as a single arrow
129	so the existing `Arrow.augmentedCechNerve` machinery applies. -/
130	noncomputable def coverArrow (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) : Arrow Scheme.{u} :=
131	  Arrow.mk (Sigma.desc 𝒰.f)
132	
133	/-- The augmented {\v C}ech nerve of an open cover `𝒰`, as an augmented
134	simplicial scheme over `X`: in simplicial degree `p` it is the `(p+1)`-fold
135	fibre power of `∐ᵢ Uᵢ` over `X`, i.e. `∐_{(i₀,…,i_p)} U_{i₀} ×ₓ ⋯ ×ₓ U_{i_p}`,
136	with augmentation the cover map to `X`. Exists unconditionally because `Scheme`
137	has all finite limits (hence the wide pullbacks used by
138	`Arrow.augmentedCechNerve`). Project-local geometric backbone for `CechNerve`. -/
139	noncomputable def coverCechNerve (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) :
140	    SimplicialObject.Augmented Scheme.{u} :=
141	  (coverArrow 𝒰).augmentedCechNerve

144	plumbing). Given `f : X ⟶ S` and an augmented cosimplicial object `N` of
145	`O_X`-modules, produce the relative {\v C}ech cochain complex in `QCoh(S)` by:
146	forgetting the augmentation (`CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop`), pushing the
147	cosimplicial object forward along `f` (`CosimplicialObject.whiskering` applied to
148	`Scheme.Modules.pushforward f`), and taking the alternating coface-map cochain

152	noncomputable def relativeCechComplexOfNerve (f : X ⟶ S)
153	    (N : CosimplicialObject.Augmented X.Modules) : CochainComplex S.Modules ℕ :=
154	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex S.Modules).obj
155	    (((CosimplicialObject.whiskering X.Modules S.Modules).obj
156	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f)).obj (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj N))
157	
158	/-- **Relative Čech complex of a quasi-coherent sheaf** (Stacks, Cohomology of
159	Schemes, `lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`).
160	
161	For `f : X ⟶ S`, a finite affine open cover `𝒰` of `X` (with all intersections
162	affine, e.g. `X` separated) and a quasi-coherent sheaf `F`, the *relative Čech
163	complex* `Č•(𝒰, F)` is the cochain complex of `O_S`-modules with degree-`p` term
164	```
165	  Čᵖ(𝒰, F) = ∏_{(i₀,…,i_p)} (f|_{U_{i₀…i_p}})_* (F|_{U_{i₀…i_p}}),
166	```
167	and differential the alternating sum of the restriction maps
168	`(d s)_{i₀…i_{p+1}} = Σⱼ (-1)ʲ s_{i₀…î_j…i_{p+1}}|_{U_{i₀…i_{p+1}}}`. Over an
169	affine `U = Spec A` with `F|_U = M~` and a standard cover by the `D(fᵢ)`, this is
170	the complex of localisations `∏ M_{f_{i₀}} → ∏ M_{f_{i₀}f_{i₁}} → ⋯`. Each term
171	is quasi-coherent because the intersections are affine and the pushforward of a
172	quasi-coherent sheaf along a quasi-compact quasi-separated morphism is
173	quasi-coherent.
174	
175	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
176	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`. -/
177	noncomputable def CechComplex (f : X ⟶ S) (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
178	    CochainComplex S.Modules ℕ :=
179	  -- Construction (Stacks): apply the relative pushforward `f_*` over each finite
180	  -- intersection to the Čech nerve `CechNerve 𝒰 F`, then take the alternating-sum
181	  -- Čech differential. This is exactly the coherence-free plumbing
182	  -- `relativeCechComplexOfNerve`, so `CechComplex` is genuinely *defined* in terms
183	  -- of the nerve: an axiom-clean `CechNerve` immediately yields an axiom-clean
184	  -- `CechComplex`. The only remaining hole is `CechNerve` itself.
185	  relativeCechComplexOfNerve f (CechNerve 𝒰 F)

187	/-- **Čech acyclicity on affines** (Stacks 02KG;
188	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial` and
189	`lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero`).
190	
191	Let `X = Spec A` be affine, `F` a quasi-coherent `O_X`-module, and `𝒰` a finite
192	standard open cover (the `fᵢ ∈ A` generate the unit ideal). Then the relative
193	Čech complex (here with `f` an affine morphism) has vanishing cohomology in all
194	positive degrees: `Hᵖ = 0` for `p ≥ 1`, equivalently `Hᵖ(X, F) = 0` for `p > 0`
195	(Serre vanishing for quasi-coherent sheaves on affines).
196	
197	The proof (Stacks): write `F|_X = M~`; the Čech complex of the standard cover is
198	the complex of localisations, and `Hᵖ = 0` for `p > 0` is equivalent to exactness
199	of the extended complex `0 → M → ∏ M_{f_{i₀}} → ⋯`. Exactness is checked after
200	localising at an arbitrary prime `𝔭`; choosing `i_fix` with `f_{i_fix} ∉ 𝔭`, the
201	prescription `h(s)_{i₀…i_p} = s_{i_fix i₀…i_p}` is a contracting homotopy, so the
202	localised complex is exact, hence so is the complex. The Čech-to-cohomology
203	comparison on the basis of affine opens then gives the sheaf statement. -/
204	theorem CechAcyclic.affine [IsAffine X] (f : X ⟶ S) [IsAffineHom f]
205	    (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
206	    (F : X.Modules) (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent) (p : ℕ) (hp : 1 ≤ p) :
207	    IsZero ((CechComplex f 𝒰 F).homology p) := by
208	  -- Proof (Stacks 02KG): on the affine `X = Spec A` the Čech complex of the

\subsection{The three-part construction of the relative {\v C}ech complex}
\label{sec:cech_three_part}

It is convenient to factor the construction of
Definitions~\ref{def:cech_nerve}--\ref{def:cech_complex} into three independent
pieces. Two of them are purely formal and unconditional; the entire mathematical
content that is not yet available off the shelf is isolated in the middle piece.

\paragraph{(1) Geometric backbone — the augmented {\v C}ech nerve of an arrow.}
Package the affine cover as a single morphism
\(\coprod_{i \in I} U_i \longrightarrow X\) (the canonical map out of the
coproduct induced by the inclusions \(U_i \hookrightarrow X\)). The augmented
{\v C}ech nerve of this arrow is an augmented \emph{simplicial scheme} over
\(X\): its object in simplicial degree \(p\) is the \((p+1)\)-fold fibre power
\[
  \Bigl(\coprod_{i} U_i\Bigr)^{\times_X (p+1)}
    \;=\;
    \coprod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
      U_{i_0} \times_X \cdots \times_X U_{i_p},
\]
with the augmentation the cover map to \(X\). Because the \(U_i\) are open
subschemes of \(X\), each fibre product \(U_{i_0} \times_X \cdots \times_X U_{i_p}\)
is canonically the intersection \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\). This backbone exists
\emph{unconditionally}: it uses only that the category of schemes has coproducts
and finite limits (hence the wide pullbacks underlying the {\v C}ech nerve of an
arrow). It carries no sheaf-theoretic data yet — it is the simplicial diagram of
spaces over which the construction is indexed.

\paragraph{(2) Push--pull functor — lifting the backbone to modules.}
The geometric backbone is turned into a cosimplicial \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module by
the \emph{relative-direct-image functor on the over-category}
\[
  G : (X/\mathbf{Sch})^{\mathrm{op}} \longrightarrow \operatorname{QCoh}(X),
  \qquad
  (Y \xrightarrow{\,p\,} X) \;\longmapsto\; p_*\, p^*\mathcal{F},
\]
sending an \(X\)-scheme \(p : Y \to X\) to the pushforward along \(p\) of the
pullback \(p^*\mathcal{F}\). Composing \(G\) with the (opposite of the) simplicial
backbone of (1) produces exactly the augmented cosimplicial object of
Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve}: on the degree-\(p\) piece
\(p : U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \hookrightarrow X\) one has
\(p_* p^*\mathcal{F} = (j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}})\),
and a morphism of \(X\)-schemes \(h : (Y_1, p_1) \to (Y_2, p_2)\) (so
\(h \circ p_2 = p_1\)\,, reading \(p\) as the structure map) induces the
restriction comparison \(G(p_2) \to G(p_1)\) via the unit/counit of the
pull-push adjunction together with the over-triangle identifying the two
structure maps. This functor \(G\) is the \emph{one non-formal step} in the whole
construction. Its functoriality —- that \(G\) respects identities and composition
of morphisms over \(X\) —- is precisely a statement about the coherence of the
comparison isomorphisms \((p \circ q)_* \cong p_* q_*\) and
\((p \circ q)^* \cong q^* p^*\) for pushforward and pullback of sheaves of
modules, together with their compatibility with the adjunction units. This is
exactly the same pushforward/pullback coherence machinery developed for the
tensor--pullback substrate (the comparison isomorphisms
\(\mathrm{pushforwardComp}\), \(\mathrm{pullbackComp}\) and the unit-transport
identities of that development): the functoriality of the relative-direct-image
functor is a \emph{consumer} of that coherence, not an independent construction.

\paragraph{(3) Coherence-free plumbing — from the nerve to the cochain complex.}
The passage from the augmented cosimplicial \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module of (1)--(2)
to the relative {\v C}ech complex in \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\) is again purely
formal and uses \emph{no} pushforward/pullback coherence. One forgets the
augmentation, pushes the cosimplicial object forward along \(f : X \to S\) (a
covariant operation applied degreewise), and takes the alternating-coface-map
cochain complex of the resulting cosimplicial object of \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-modules.
This step relies only on the preadditivity of \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\): the
alternating sum \(\sum_{j} (-1)^j d^j\) of the cofaces is defined in any
preadditive category and squares to zero by the cosimplicial identities. Thus the
relative {\v C}ech complex \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\)
of Definition~\ref{def:cech_complex} is genuinely \emph{defined} in terms of the
{\v C}ech nerve of Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve}: once the nerve is constructed,
the complex follows with no further input. In summary, of the three pieces only
the push--pull functor (2) requires content beyond formal category theory, and
that content is the pushforward/pullback coherence shared with the
tensor--pullback substrate.
